title: Kangaroo Level 3-4 - Practice Test 1
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: What is the value of 17 + 28 - 9?
Lena has 12 stickers. She gives 3 stickers to each of her 2 friends. How many stickers does she have left?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><g stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='15' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='35' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='55' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='75' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='95' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='115' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='135' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='155' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='175' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='195' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='215' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='235' cy='30' r='8'/></g></svg>
questions.solution: She gives away 3 x 2 = 6 stickers. 12 - 6 = 6 stickers remain.

questions.question_text: How many small squares are shaded in the grid shown?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='10' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='none' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='70' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='10' y='40' width='30' height='30' fill='none' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='40' y='40' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='70' y='40' width='30' height='30' fill='none' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='10' y='70' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='40' y='70' width='30' height='30' fill='none' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='70' y='70' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A 3x3 grid with shaded squares in a checkered pattern starting at top-left.
questions.solution: Counting the shaded squares in the checkered pattern gives 5 shaded squares.

questions.question_text: A pencil costs 2 dollars and an eraser costs 1 dollar. Tom buys 3 pencils and 2 erasers. How much does he pay?
questions.solution: 3 x 2 = 6 dollars for pencils, 2 x 1 = 2 dollars for erasers. Total is 6 + 2 = 8 dollars.

questions.question_text: Anna is taller than Ben. Ben is taller than Carl. Who is the shortest?
questions.solution: Since Anna > Ben > Carl in height, Carl is the shortest.

questions.question_text: What is the perimeter of the rectangle shown, with sides of 6 cm and 4 cm?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='20' y='30' width='120' height='60' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='70' y='25' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>6 cm</text><text x='145' y='65' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>4 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle with a horizontal side labeled 6 cm and vertical side labeled 4 cm.
questions.solution: Perimeter = 2 x (6 + 4) = 2 x 10 = 20 cm.

questions.question_text: Which of these numbers is even?
questions.solution: An even number ends in 0, 2, 4, 6, or 8. Only 42 ends in 2, so 42 is even.

questions.question_text: A bus leaves at 9:15 and arrives at 10:45. How long is the trip in minutes?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><circle cx='50' cy='55' r='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='50' y1='55' x2='50' y2='25' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='50' y1='55' x2='68' y2='55' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='30' y='105' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>9:15</text><circle cx='170' cy='55' r='40' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='170' y1='55' x2='170' y2='85' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='170' y1='55' x2='188' y2='55' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='148' y='105' font-size='12' fill='currentColor'>10:45</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Two clocks showing 9:15 on the left and 10:45 on the right.
questions.solution: From 9:15 to 10:15 is 60 minutes, plus 30 more minutes = 90 minutes total.

questions.question_text: Three children share 24 candies equally. Then each child gives 2 candies back. How many candies does each child have now?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><g stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='20'/><circle cx='120' cy='40' r='20'/><circle cx='200' cy='40' r='20'/></g><text x='32' y='45' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>C1</text><text x='112' y='45' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>C2</text><text x='192' y='45' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>C3</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three children labeled C1, C2, C3.
questions.solution: Each child first gets 24 / 3 = 8 candies. After giving back 2, each has 8 - 2 = 6 candies.

questions.question_text: In a row of 5 houses, the red house is next to the blue house. The blue house is in the middle. The green house is at the far left. Which house is next to the green house?
questions.solution: Position 3 is blue, position 1 is green. The house next to green is position 2, which must be yellow or white (not red or blue, since red is next to blue at position 4).

questions.question_text: The shape shown is made from squares of side 1 cm. What is its area?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 150 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='10' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='70' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='10' y='40' width='30' height='30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='10' y='70' width='30' height='30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='40' y='70' width='30' height='30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: An L-shaped figure composed of 6 unit squares.
questions.solution: Counting the unit squares in the L-shape gives 6 squares, so the area is 6 sq cm.

questions.question_text: Find the missing digit: 3 _ 7 + 145 = 482.
questions.solution: 482 - 145 = 337, so the missing digit is 3.

questions.question_text: A box contains red, blue, and green marbles. There are 5 red marbles. There are twice as many blue as red. There are 3 fewer green than blue. How many marbles are in the box?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='10' y='10' width='180' height='100' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='20' y='35' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>Red: 5</text><text x='20' y='60' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>Blue: 2 x Red</text><text x='20' y='85' font-size='14' fill='currentColor'>Green: Blue - 3</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A box illustration with a summary of marble counts.

questions.question_text: Three boxes each have a label. One says 'apples', one says 'oranges', and one says 'mixed'. All three labels are wrong. You pick one fruit from the box labeled 'mixed' and it is an apple. What is actually in the box labeled 'oranges'?
questions.solution: Since all labels are wrong and the 'mixed' box gave an apple, it must be apples only. The 'oranges' box cannot contain oranges, and cannot be apples only (already taken), so it must be mixed.

questions.question_text: The triangle shown is divided into equal smaller triangles. How many small triangles does it contain in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='100,20 20,160 180,160' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='60' y1='90' x2='140' y2='90' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='60' y1='90' x2='100' y2='160' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='140' y1='90' x2='100' y2='160' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle divided into 4 smaller equal triangles by connecting midpoints of the sides.
questions.solution: Connecting the midpoints of a triangle's sides creates 4 smaller congruent triangles.

questions.question_text: Maya is 9 years old. Her brother is 4 years younger. How old will her brother be when Maya is 15?
questions.solution: Maya's brother is 9 - 4 = 5 now. When Maya is 15, 6 years will pass, so her brother will be 5 + 6 = 11.

questions.question_text: Five friends sit in a row. Eva is not at either end. Fay sits immediately to the right of Eva. Gus sits at the left end. Who can sit at the right end?
questions.solution: Gus is at seat 1. Eva must be at 2, 3, or 4. Fay is immediately right of Eva. Eva cannot be at seat 5. So the right end (seat 5) is occupied by either Hal or Ivy (the remaining friends).

questions.question_text: A farmer has chickens and cows. Together they have 20 heads and 56 legs. How many cows does he have?
questions.solution: If all 20 were chickens: 40 legs. Extra legs: 56 - 40 = 16. Each cow adds 2 legs, so cows = 16 / 2 = 8.

questions.question_text: The figure shows a square divided by two lines. What fraction of the square is shaded?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='20' y='20' width='100' height='100' fill='none' stroke='currentColor'/><polygon points='20,20 120,20 120,70 20,70' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='20' y1='120' x2='120' y2='120' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='20' y1='70' x2='120' y2='70' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A square is divided by a horizontal line into two rectangles; the top half is shaded.
questions.solution: The horizontal line divides the square into two equal rectangles. The shaded one is half, so 1/2.

questions.question_text:
In the magic square, every row, column, and diagonal sums to 15. What number replaces the question mark?

2 | 9 | ?
7 | 5 | 3
6 | 1 | 8
Four cards are placed in a row. Each card has a different color: red, yellow, blue, green. Red is not first. Blue is immediately before green. Yellow is last. What color is second?
questions.solution: Yellow is 4th. Blue-Green must be consecutive in positions 1-2 or 2-3. Red is not first. If Blue-Green at 1-2, Red is 3rd - works. Position 2 is Green.

questions.question_text: A book has 180 pages. Lily reads 15 pages each day. On what day does she finish the book if she starts on Monday?
questions.solution: 180 / 15 = 12 days. Starting Monday (day 1), day 12 is: Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri, Sat, Sun, Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri. She finishes on Friday.

questions.question_text: The shape shown is made of 5 identical squares with side 2 cm. What is the perimeter of the shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='10' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='70' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='40' y='40' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='40' y='70' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A plus-sign shape made of 5 squares: 3 in the top row and a column of 2 more extending down from the middle square.
questions.solution: The shape has 12 exposed unit edges, each of length 2 cm. Perimeter = 12 x 2 = 24 cm.

questions.question_text: Using the digits 1, 2, 3, 4 each exactly once, how many different 4-digit even numbers can be formed?
questions.solution: The last digit must be 2 or 4 (even). For each choice, the remaining 3 digits can be arranged in 3! = 6 ways. Total = 2 x 6 = 12.
